question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `EnumeratedDistribution<TransactionGenerator>` in `CompositeTransactionGenerator` to weight a computationally expensive transaction type at 100% probability, causing the network node to exclusively process high-cost transactions and increasing its resource consumption by at least 30% above baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `RateLimiter.create(tps, warmupPeriod)` in `CompositeTransactionGenerator.getRateLimiter()` to configure a very long warmup period, causing the rate limiter to allow a large burst of transactions at startup that overwhelms the network node and increases its resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `rebuild()` method in `CompositeTransactionGenerator` being called on `ScenarioException` to repeatedly disable and re-enable scenarios, causing the rate limiter to be reset and allowing burst traffic to the network node each time, increasing its resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `current.setRate(total)` call in `CompositeTransactionGenerator.rebuild()` to dynamically increase the TPS rate of the rate limiter without restarting the monitor, causing a sudden increase in transaction submission rate to the network node and raising its resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Flux.generate` loop combined with `flatMapIterable` to cause unbounded backpressure accumulation in the reactive pipeline, forcing the network node to process a burst of queued transactions when backpressure is released, increasing its resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `sendRecord=true` flag in `PublishRequest` to cause `processTransactionResponse()` to execute a `TransactionRecordQuery` (which internally does a receipt query, cost query, and record query — three separate network node calls) for every transaction, tripling the per-transaction node load and increasing resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `secureRandom.nextInt(publishProperties.getClients())` client selection in `TransactionPublisher.publish()` to cause uneven load distribution across clients, with some clients sending disproportionately more transactions to specific network nodes, increasing those nodes' resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodeSupplier.get()` call in `getTransactionResponse()` to cause all transactions to be routed to a single network node (e.g., by manipulating node health scores), concentrating resource consumption on that node and increasing it by at least 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `clients.cache()` Flux in `TransactionPublisher` so that all cached clients share the same node endpoint configuration, causing all parallel publisher threads to target the same network node and increasing its resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Mono.fromFuture(executable.executeAsync(client))` async execution path to submit transactions faster than the network node can process them, causing the node's transaction queue to grow and its memory/CPU consumption to increase by at least 30% above the 24-hour baseline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `statusFrequency` default of 10 seconds in `PublishProperties` to avoid detection of elevated transaction rates for up to 10 seconds, allowing a burst of expensive transactions to reach the network node and increase its resource consumption by at least 30% before any alerting occurs?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ConcurrentHashMap<Tags, Timer>` in `PublishMetrics` by causing a large number of unique tag combinations (e.g., many distinct node/scenario/status combinations) to be created, causing unbounded memory growth in the monitor that indirectly degrades its ability to throttle transactions and increases network node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `onErrorMap(t -> !(t instanceof PublishException), t -> new PublishException(request, t))` in `TransactionPublisher.publish()` to cause non-PublishException errors to be wrapped and re-thrown, bypassing the `onErrorContinue` handler and causing the Flux to restart via `.retry()`, flooding the network node with repeated transaction attempts and increasing its resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `NodeAddressBook` construction in `TransactionPublisher.toClient()` to inject duplicate or malformed node addresses that cause the SDK to attempt connections to multiple endpoints per transaction, increasing the network node's connection-handling overhead by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `(a, b) -> a` merge function in `Collectors.toMap()` within `TransactionPublisher.toClient()` to cause endpoint deduplication that routes all traffic to a single network node, concentrating resource consumption on that node and increasing it by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `client.setNodeMaxBackoff(publishProperties.getNodeMaxBackoff())` configuration to set a very long max backoff, causing the SDK to retry failed transactions against the network node for up to 1 minute each, keeping the node busy processing repeated expensive transactions and increasing its resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Hooks.onErrorDropped` static initializer that suppresses `CompletionException` stack traces to hide evidence of a flood of async transaction errors, allowing the monitor to continue submitting failing transactions to the network node without operator awareness, increasing node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@ConditionalOnProperty(value = 'hiero.mirror.monitor.publish.enabled', havingValue = 'true', matchIfMissing = true)` default-enabled behavior so that the publish flow starts automatically without explicit operator opt-in, and by manipulating the scenario configuration can cause the network node's resource consumption to increase by at least 30%?
